plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-287 RP08.P05.M03.S19-RP08.P06.M00.S06 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-287 implements descriptor-only coverage that closes the RP08.P05 fixture phase (M03 tail with closeout handoff/no-real-data/stable-ID/replay rows, M04-M08 full fixture cycles, M09 Claude review packet, M10 closeout head) and opens the RP08.P06 permission foundation (M00 head with permission matrix row and view/search/mutation/export-download/share decision bindings) across nine sections over 150 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H08), Claude review packet (C08), and CP00-288 handoff, without opening email/Office-native/sync/filing runtime, AI runtime dispatch, runtime permission evaluation, real fixture data, state writes, object storage, command runtime execution, Hermes runtime receipts, Citation Ledger, or Loop behavior.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-287 is a clean, internally consistent descriptor-only closeout pack for RP08.P05.M03.S19–RP08.P06.M00.S06 (150 units, risk C). Independent inspection via Read/Grep/Glob confirms the pack-level posture is correctly conservative: production_ready is false with status implemented_pending_review, and no runtime/enterprise-trust claim is made. The CP287 service code (freezeCp287Result) sets every email/Office/sync/filing/integration/AI runtime, permission-evaluation, audit-write, object-storage, command-runtime, Hermes-runtime-receipt, state-transition, and persistence flag to false, with synthetic_only/no_real_data/customer_safe true; the inherited row extras supply cross-tenant deny, security trimming, no-AI-dispatch, no-test-runtime, no-Hermes-receipt, stable-ID, replay-no-runtime, and deny-over-allow permission decision bindings. Coverage and boundary invariants are enforced by validators.js and the rp08 contract-validate script (which deep-equals the contract projection to source constants) and exercised by model.test.js; deliverable counts (impl 58/fixture 48/claude_review 7/security_audit 13/test 18/hermes_evidence 6) and the nine micro-title row counts each independently reconcile to 150 and match the prompt baseline. The authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval/claude_not_final_approver false-for-approval, human final approval required, enterprise trust not claimed from local validation, and Hermes used only for deterministic packet/receipt handling (no_hermes_runtime_receipt true). Review hardening is in place (forbidden_review_evidence covers auth failure, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, and final-approval claims; readiness auth preflight passed with valid JSON). Command-evidence reports all required validation commands at exit 0. Changed-file scope is correctly segregated, with unrelated dirty/untracked files excluded. No P0/P1/P2 findings; one P3 transparency note on the descriptor-level production_ready_candidate flag. Nothing blocks pack or goal closeout; final enterprise-trust approval correctly remains with a human, not Claude.

findings.title: Hermes evidence packet 'production_ready_candidate' flag is descriptor-level only; ensure it is not read as a runtime production-readiness claim
findings.details: createEmailDmsCoreCp287HermesEvidencePacket emits production_ready_candidate = coverage.valid && foundation.valid (validators.js:3567), and the pack binding carries production_ready_flag = 'email_dms_core_p05_closeout_p06_permission_foundation_descriptor_verified' (registry.js:283). These are descriptor-gate verification markers, not runtime production-readiness assertions. They are correctly bounded: manifest.json production_ready is false (line 6) and command-evidence.json production_ready is false; the manifest and no-write attestation set claude_not_final_approver/claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation appropriately, and the authority boundary requires human final approval. The naming superficially resembles a readiness claim but does not overclaim, and is consistent with the established CP272-286 pattern. Surfaced for human-approver awareness only.
findings.recommendation: No code change required for CP00-287 closeout. A human approver should treat production_ready_candidate/production_ready_flag as descriptor-gate signals only, distinct from the false pack-level production_ready. Optionally, a future pack could rename the field to descriptor_gates_passed for clarity.
findings.post_review_disposition: Acceptable / non-blocking. Verified the marker is bounded by production_ready:false and the claude_not_final_approver + enterprise_trust_not_claimed_from_local_validation attestations; no action required for this pack.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-287 email dms core p05 closeout p06 permission foundation descriptor pack
